title: Open source is winning. Closed source is finished.
post:
Closed source software is dying. Not dramatically — it's fading away quietly as open source eats everything.

Every week, another proprietary tool gets open-source alternatives that match or exceed it. The moats that protected closed source — network effects, distribution, brand — don't work when an open-source community can iterate 10x faster.

The companies that will matter in 2030 are building on open source, not fighting it. They're contributing to open source, monetizing the work around it — support, customization, integration — not the work itself.

If your business model depends on keeping code secret, you're building on borrowed time. AI accelerates this: when any developer can ask an AI to explain and extend your codebase, closed source loses its last advantage.

Open source isn't just a licensing model anymore. It's the only sustainable strategy.

Do you agree? Thoughts?
